question: Assess whether an API-only replacement exists for counting topic views, based on Discourse behavior and browser navigation requirements. Return concise JSON with recommendation, evidence, risks.
result_json: {"recommendation":"不要把“主帖浏览量计数”替换成纯 API 调用。在 stock Discourse/Linux.do 上，可靠路径仍应是真浏览器进入话题路由/页面并触发 browser pageview；`/topics/timings` 可用于阅读计时或已读进度，但不能视为等价的 topic view 计数 API。若必须 API 化，只能由站点侧插件/服务端改造显式支持。","confidence":"高","evidence":["仓库现状：`autobrowser.py` 通过真实浏览器中键打开话题页并滚动；`userscripts/flowreader.user.js` 的首页主帖逻辑仅 POST `/topics/timings`，参数是 `topic_id`、`topic_time`、`timings[1]`。","Discourse 官方 Meta 讨论明确说明：REST/API 请求不会更新 topic views；topic views 已迁移到 browser pageviews，API/crawler 请求不计入。来源：Discourse Meta `Topic views doesn't update on rest API requests`。","Discourse 源码 `TopicsController#timings` 只调用 `PostTiming.process_timings(...)`，处理帖子阅读时长；未调用 `defer_topic_view`。","Discourse 源码 `RequestTracker` 只有在 `tracks_browser_page_view?` 成立且存在 `topic_id` 时才调用 `TopicsController.defer_topic_view(...)`；`/topics/timings` 被标记为 background 类型，和 topic view 计数链路不同。","源码注释说明 `Discourse-Track-View` 来自 Ember 路由导航，deferred/beacon pageview 依赖真实浏览器加载后的后续请求；这与普通 API key 或后台请求不同。"],"risks":["伪造 browser pageview 相关 header/Beacon 属于私有实现细节，可能被版本、站点配置、CSRF、Same-Origin、登录态、反滥用策略或 crawler 识别破坏。","即使 `/topics/timings` 返回成功，也只能说明计时/已读请求被接受，不能证明 topic view 递增。","Linux.do 是第三方站点，无法部署 Discourse 插件时，不存在可控的服务端 API 兜底。","真实浏览器导航成本更高，但更符合 Discourse 当前计数语义；纯 API 替换会带来静默失效风险。"],"next_steps":["保留 Python/DrissionPage 的真实话题打开路径作为浏览量计数的可靠方案。","将 UserScript 首页“浏览主帖”的语义标注为阅读计时/进度辅助，不承诺增加 Discourse topic views；必要时调整 UI 文案和 README。","若要验证 Linux.do 实际行为，做小样本 A/B：记录 topic views 初值，分别执行 `/topics/timings` 与真实打开话题，等待去重窗口/后台任务后比较。","若拥有站点服务端控制权，评估插件方案：在受控 API 入口调用等价的 topic view 记录逻辑，并加限流、权限和去重。"]}

topic: FlowReader userscript current-tab main topic browsing
question: Assess implementation options in this repo: current-tab left click flow, location navigation, CDP/DrissionPage, and likely safest path. Return concise JSON with recommendation, files, risks.
result_json: {"recommendation":"Safest path: keep this in `userscripts/flowreader.user.js` and implement current-tab browsing as a small persisted userscript state machine, not via CDP/DrissionPage. Prefer deterministic `window.location.assign(topic.url)`/same-tab navigation with saved queue/index over synthetic DOM left-click; use existing SPA route handling to reinitialize on topic pages, then mark the main post and continue/return. Keep existing direct `/topics/timings` flow as fallback or legacy mode if the goal is only marking visible main posts read.","confidence":"medium-high","files":["userscripts/flowreader.user.js","userscripts/tests/flowreader.user.test.js","autobrowser.py","main.py","docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-07-flowreader-spa-route-reinit-design.md"],"evidence":["`collectHomeTopicItems` already extracts ordered, de-duplicated Linux.do topic IDs and canonical URLs, so a queue can reuse existing parsed data instead of adding new DOM discovery.","`startReadingMainTopics` currently stays on the home page and sends `sendMainTopicTiming(topic.id)` directly; it has progress storage, retry, failure cutoff, and button state that can be reused but it does not currently perform real current-tab navigation.","`handleRouteChange` already distinguishes home/topic routes, cleans old UI, waits for topic context on topic pages, and is covered by tests for home init and topic reinit/autostart; this is the right hook for navigation-based continuation.","`autobrowser.py`/`main.py` are Python DrissionPage automation paths using `click.middle()` to open new tabs, scroll, close tabs, and run concurrent tasks. Switching that to current-tab left clicks would require back/forward state, stale element recovery, and real-browser smoke coverage.","Existing repo constraints say FlowReader is a single-file native userscript and SPA route handling must remain idempotent; changing CDP/DrissionPage is a wider architecture boundary than this feature needs."],"risks":["Synthetic left click (`element.click()`/MouseEvent) may not be treated as trusted user input and may interact poorly with Discourse delegated routing; it is less deterministic than assigning `location`.","`location.assign` may full-reload instead of SPA-navigate, so continuation must be stored before navigation and be resilient to reload, login state, and homepage scroll/list changes.","Current direct POST behavior marks main post timing without actually visiting pages; replacing it outright could change user-visible behavior and verification scope.","A navigation queue can loop or skip items if route initialization fails, topic context times out, or the user manually navigates during a run; needs explicit running/paused/error state.","DrissionPage/CDP changes are higher risk because 4.2 is beta-verified locally, existing browser smoke is not default, and current automation uses new-tab concurrency assumptions."],"next_steps":["Add a GM storage key for main-topic navigation session: source route, topic queue, current index, status, and last error; keep the existing progress ID storage separate.","Refactor `startReadingMainTopics` so it can either run the existing direct timing mode or start a same-tab navigation session from `collectHomeTopicItems`.","On topic route initialization, if a main-topic navigation session is active, call the existing main-post timing request for that topic, record progress, then navigate to the next queued topic or back to the source route when done.","Add Node tests for queue normalization, session advance/stop behavior, route-driven continuation, and manual interruption cleanup; then run `node --check userscripts/flowreader.user.js` and `node --test userscripts/tests/flowreader.user.test.js`.","Avoid DrissionPage/CDP unless the requirement explicitly moves from userscript behavior to browser automation; if that happens, plan a separate Python change with real Edge smoke verification."]}

question: Assess focused test strategy for replacing /topics/timings homepage browsing with current-tab navigation/session state. Return concise JSON with tests and residual risk.
result_json: {"recommendation":"Use a focused layered test suite: keep pure Node tests for route recognition, topic collection, progress/session normalization, and add orchestration tests around injected navigation/storage/fetch seams to prove current-tab browsing never calls /topics/timings, advances only after successful topic-page handling, survives reload/route changes, and stops safely on missing context or repeated failures. Add one manual or browser smoke test on linux.do/latest because Node fake DOM cannot fully model Discourse SPA navigation.","confidence":"medium-high","evidence":["userscripts/flowreader.user.js currently has clear seams for route handling: getRouteSignature, handleRouteChange, waitForPageContext, cleanupScriptUI, setupUI, autoStart branch.","Existing tests already cover home route signatures, supported homepage detection, topic URL parsing, home route initialization not waiting for timeline/autostarting replies, visible topic collection, read-ID normalization/capping, skip-read behavior, button labels, failure threshold, and /topics/timings request params.","Current homepage browsing path still centers on sendMainTopicTiming/startReadingMainTopics posting to /topics/timings, so replacing it with current-tab navigation needs new tests for navigation side effects and session state transitions, not more parameter tests.","README describes intended user behavior: homepage/latest browse visible main topics, continue after scrolling, persist up to 20000 read topic IDs, reset progress, and topic pages retain reply-reading/export actions."],"risks":["Node unit tests with fake documents will not catch real Discourse DOM changes, History API/popstate timing, Tampermonkey storage quirks, or sessionStorage persistence across full navigations.","Current-tab navigation can interrupt async code; tests must verify state is persisted before navigation and that duplicate route-change events are idempotent.","A failed waitForPageContext or missing CSRF/timeline on a topic page could otherwise mark a topic read or skip it incorrectly.","Auto-start reply reading on topic routes may conflict with main-topic browsing unless session state distinguishes homepage browsing from normal topic reading."],"next_steps":["Add pure tests for session object creation, corrupt/expired session recovery, queue advancement, read-ID merge, and reset behavior.","Add mocked orchestration tests: home click builds unread queue and calls injected navigate(url); topic route with active session waits context, performs the chosen read action, marks current topic read only on success, then navigates to next or clears session.","Add negative tests: no topics, all topics read, invalid external links, missing CSRF/timeline timeout, duplicate handleRouteChange, and repeated failure stop without advancing.","Run existing Node userscript tests plus a small browser/manual smoke on /latest to verify current-tab navigation, UI remount, and resume behavior."]}
